spoilers.title: #ONEPIECE1175 In the latest spoiler, Ratatoskr is said to be the "Ice Squirrel" Ragnir = Ratatoskr = Ice Powers❄️ So Loki = Nidhogg = Lightning Powers ⚡️ SO... DID LOKI REALLY USED HIS DF POWERS IN TH
spoilers.text: #ONEPIECE1175 In the latest spoiler, Ratatoskr is said to be the "Ice Squirrel" Ragnir = Ratatoskr = Ice Powers❄️ So Loki = Nidhogg = Lightning Powers ⚡️ SO... DID LOKI REALLY USED HIS DF POWERS IN THIS SCENE WHILE STILL BEING CHAINED WITH SEASTONE?! 👀

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1175 HMMM WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1175 When the God of War passed away, legend says Ratatsokr’s spirit took residence within the hammer. It guarded its master’s Devil Fruit, holding
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1175 HMMM WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1175 When the God of War passed away, legend says Ratatsokr’s spirit took residence within the hammer. It guarded its master’s Devil Fruit, holding onto it until a successor strong and worthy enough would finally claim it. — https://nitter.net/WorstGenHQ/status/2026310133043609912#m

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1175 So the special bounty can't be because of his df, otherwise Imu and the WG would know it. So why Loki got the special bounty? Did anyone in the new world saw his df power except Shanks?
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1175 So the special bounty can't be because of his df, otherwise Imu and the WG would know it. So why Loki got the special bounty? Did anyone in the new world saw his df power except Shanks? Imagine he gained it just by staying always in base form💀💀💀

misc.title: In Norse mythology, Nidhogg (whose name roughly means “the one who crouches in burning rage”) is said to gnaw at the third root of Yggdrasil. When you look at that, it kind of feels like it connects t
misc.text: In Norse mythology, Nidhogg (whose name roughly means “the one who crouches in burning rage”) is said to gnaw at the third root of Yggdrasil. When you look at that, it kind of feels like it connects to the line in Harley that says, “The Earth God, enraged…”🤔 #ONEPIECE

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1175 When the God of War passed away, legend says Ratatsokr’s spirit took residence within the hammer. It guarded its master’s Devil Fruit, holding onto it until a successor strong and worthy
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1175 When the God of War passed away, legend says Ratatsokr’s spirit took residence within the hammer. It guarded its master’s Devil Fruit, holding onto it until a successor strong and worthy enough would finally claim it.

items.text: In Norse mythology, Nidhogg (whose name roughly means “the one who crouches in burning rage”) is said to gnaw at the third root of Yggdrasil. When you look at that, it kind of feels like it connects to the line in Harley that says, “The Earth God, enraged…”🤔 #ONEPIECE
